This Week's Popular Listings

1966 Bill Thomas Cheetah Coupe

USA
P Premium
This Bill Thomas Cheetah is believed to be the last example constructed and was commissioned without a drivetrain in November 1965 by Eddie King, who accepted delivery in April 1966 at King's Sales and Service Inc. in Ohio. King registered the car for street use and retained ownership through 1968, when its second steward acquired the vehicle in a…
262
61,114 Views
Sold for USD $520,000 on 4/14/2026

1995 Porsche 911 Carrera Speedster by Gunther Werks

USA
P Premium
A Alumni
This 1995 Porsche 911 Carrera cabriolet was previously sold on BaT in September 2021 before being delivered to Gunther Werks and modified to their Speedster specification. As one of 25 examples, the car was stripped of its convertible top, side windows, and windshield prior to receiving chassis reinforcements, a number of carbon-fiber body panels,…
84
45,472 Views
Bid to USD $850,000 on 4/16/2026

2015 Porsche 918 Spyder

USA
P Premium
This 2015 Porsche 918 Spyder is #405 of 918 examples built and was first registered in Ohio. The car is finished in Meteor Grey Metallic over Onyx Black leather with Acid Green accents and is powered by a 4.6-liter V8 that works in conjunction with dual electric motors that drive all four wheels through a seven-speed PDK automatic transaxle.
150
45,659 Views
Bid to USD $2,740,000 on 4/17/2026

1997 Porsche 911 Carrera Cup 3.8 RSR

USA
P Premium
This 1997 Porsche 911 Carrera Coupe Cup 3.8 RSR is one of approximately 45 factory-built race cars produced between 1996 and 1998 for use in American IMSA and European and International GT racing. Chassis 98077 was built on March 7, 1997 and delivered new to its first owner in Japan, where it was used in Porsche Club events in Suzuka and Fuji.
66
37,852 Views
Bid to USD $1,010,000 on 4/15/2026

USA
P Premium
This 1979 Porsche 930 Turbo is said to have been registered in Kentucky before it was acquired by the current owner in 2024. 37k-Mile 1997 Ferrari F355 Spider 6-Speed

USA
This 1997 Ferrari F355 Spider shows 37k miles and is finished in Verde Silverstone Metallic over tan Connolly leather upholstery. It is powered by a 3.5-liter DOHC V8 that received a timing belt service in December 2025, and shifting is through a six-speed manual transaxle and a limited-slip differential.

34,772 Views
Sold for USD $160,355 on 4/14/2026

Ex–Charlie Sheen 1998 Prevost Marathon XL Tour Bus

USA
This 1998 Prevost Marathon XL was custom-ordered by actor Charlie Sheen and is said to have been used as a dressing room during the production of various projects including the television series, Two and a Half Men. The bus remained with Sheen from new until it was purchased by the seller, musician Tim Montana, in 2021.
